close_reason: By design. secretx-keyring is the Linux kernel keyring backend. macOS/Windows users should use secretx-desktop (which has its own inventory registrations for those platforms).
description: In secretx-keyring/src/lib.rs the inventory::submit\! call that registers KeyringBackend for URI dispatch is wrapped in #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]. On macOS and Windows, the keyring crate works (it uses Keychain Services and Credential Manager respectively), but no backend registration is submitted. A caller on macOS using secretx::from_uri("secretx:keyring:...") gets SecretError::InvalidUri with the message 'unknown backend' — not an error indicating the backend is available but not registered on this platform. This is confusing: the secretx-desktop backend covers macOS/Windows keychains, but the error message gives no hint of this. What goes wrong: a cross-platform application that uses keyring URIs will fail on non-Linux platforms with a misleading error, and the secretx umbrella crate's documentation does not call out this platform restriction. Fix: either extend the cfg guard to also register on macOS/Windows (if keyring is a superset of desktop on those platforms), or return SecretError::Unavailable with a helpful message on unsupported platforms, or document clearly in from_uri() and the crate docs that keyring URIs only dispatch on Linux.
title: KeyringBackend inventory registration gated to Linux only
